topics.topic: Explain the character set, lexical conventions of Verilog like number specifications, identifiers, keywords, value set, nets, register, vector, integer, real and time registers
Explain the expression, operator and operand
Explain the different types of operators and their usage
Explain the port declaration rules
Explain the module definition and its components in Verilog
Explain the module instantiation, inference of a component
Demonstrate the different levels of abstractions in Verilog II. Implementing logic gates using Gate Level and Data flow modelling Theory:
Explain the gate symbols, Truth tables, gate instantiations and syntaxes in Verilog

Explain the rise delay, fall delay and turnoff delay in gate level modelling Lab Experiments on gate level modelling: 1. Implement Basic Logic Gates 2. Implement Universal gates and special gates (Ex-or and Ex-nor) 3. Implement Multi input Logic Gates Theory:

Explain assignment statements and types of delays in data flow modelling Lab Experiments on dataflow modelling: 1. Implement Basic Logic Gates 2. Implement Universal gates and special gates (Ex-or and Ex-nor) 3. Implement Multi input Logic Gates III. a) Combinational Circuits Theory:
Explain the usage of conditional/decision making statements (if, if -else, if- else-if)
Explain the usage of multiway branching statements (case, casex, casez)

Explain the usage of Iterative statements (while, for, repeat, forever) Lab Experiments on dataflow modelling using above concepts: 1. Implement Adders (Half adder and Full Adder) 2. Implement Subtractors (Half Subtractor and Full Subtractor) 3. Implement 4-bit Parallel Adder 4. Implement Multiplexers (2:1, 4:1 and 8:1 MUX) 5. Implement Demultiplexers (1:2, 1:4 and 1:8 DEMUX) 6. Implement Decoders (1:2, 2:4 and 3:8 Decoder) 7. Implement Encoders (2:1, 4:2 and 8:3 encoder) 8. Implement Comparator (2-bit and 4-bit) 9. Implement 4-bit ALU b) Sequential Circuits using Behavioral modelling Theory:
Explain the behavioral level programming concept structural procedures (initial and always statements)
Explain the procedural assignments (Blocking and non-blocking assignments)
Delay based timing control and event based timing control

Explain the structure of stimulus module / test bench Lab Experiments on behavioral modelling using above concepts: 1. Implement NAND, NOR latch 2. Implement Flip Flops (JK- Flip Flop and SR-Flip flop) using edge triggering and level triggering 3. Implement Flip Flops (D- Flip Flop and T-Flip flop) using edge triggering and level triggering 4. Implement Shift Registers (SISO, SIPO) 5. Implement Shift Registers (PISO, PIPO) 6. Implement Counters (4-bit Synchronous counter and Decade counter) 7. Implement RAM and ROM Note: All the above programs should be simulated by using test benches. IV. Implement the following on FPGA board Theory:
